collections.title: Collections
collections.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this collection?
collections.unsavedChangesMessage: You have unsaved changes. Do you really want to close the panel?
collections.duplicateUnsavedMessage: You have unsaved changes. Do you want to discard them and duplicate?
collections.truncateConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to truncate all records in "{{name}}"? This includes cascade deletion of related records and files, and cannot be undone.
collections.truncateSuccess: All records in "{{name}}" have been truncated
collections.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}" and all its records? This action cannot be undone.
collections.exceptDomainsTooltip:
List of domains that are NOT allowed.
This field is disabled if "Only domains" is set.
collections.onlyDomainsTooltip:
List of domains that are ONLY allowed.
This field is disabled if "Except domains" is set.
collections.minDateTooltip: Minimum allowed date value. Leave empty for no restriction.
collections.maxDateTooltip: Maximum allowed date value. Leave empty for no restriction.
collections.noDecimalsTooltip: Existing decimal numbers will not be affected.
collections.maxSizeTooltip: Maximum content size in bytes. Leave empty for default limit.
collections.convertUrlsTooltip: Convert URLs to relative paths for portability.
collections.allowedMimeTypesTooltip: Allow files ONLY with the listed mime types. Leave empty for no restriction.
collections.protectedTooltip: It will require View API rule permissions and file token to be accessible.
collections.secretMaxSizeTooltip: Maximum size of the secret value in bytes.
collections.secretEncryptionNote: Default to ~4KB (4096 bytes). The value is encrypted and stored securely.
collections.secretWarning: Note: Secret field values are encrypted at rest and cannot be retrieved after being set. They can only be overwritten.
collections.cascadeDeleteTooltip: Whether on {{collection}} record deletion to delete also the current corresponding collection record(s).
collections.cascadeDeleteMultiTooltip: For "Multiple" relation fields the cascade delete is triggered only when all {{collection}} ids are removed from the corresponding record.
collections.minLengthTooltip: Clear the field or set it to 0 for no limit.
collections.maxLengthTooltip: Clear the field or set it to 0 to fallback to the default limit.
collections.validationPatternTooltip: All record ids have forbidden characters and unique case-insensitive (ASCII) validations.
collections.autogeneratePatternTooltip: Set and autogenerate text matching the pattern on missing record create value.

records.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this record?
records.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete the selected records? This action cannot be undone.
records.dragAndDrop: Drag and drop files here, or
records.draftAvailable: The record has previous unsaved changes.

apiDocs.authorizationDesc: Requires admin or record authorization token
apiDocs.listDescription: Fetch a paginated {{name}} records list, supporting sorting and filtering.

params.pageDesc: The page (aka. offset) of the paginated list (default to 1).
params.perPageDesc: Specify the max returned records per page (default to 30).
params.sortDescDetail: Add - / + (default) in front of the attribute for DESC / ASC order. Ex.:
params.filterSyntaxIntro: The syntax basically follows the format
params.filterOperand: could be any of the above field literal, string (single or double quoted), number, null, true, false
params.filterLike: Like/Contains (if not specified auto wraps the right string OPERAND in a "%" for wildcard match)
params.filterNotLike: NOT Like/Contains (if not specified auto wraps the right string OPERAND in a "%" for wildcard match)
params.filterGroupNote: To group and combine several expressions you could use brackets (...), && (AND) and || (OR) tokens.
params.expandDetail1: Supports up to 6-levels depth nested relations expansion.
params.expandDetail2: The expanded relations will be appended to each individual record under the expand property (eg. "expand": {"relField1": {...}, ...}).
params.expandDetail3: Only the relations to which the request user has permissions to view will be expanded.
params.fieldsDesc: Comma separated string of the fields to return in the JSON response (by default returns all fields). Ex.:
params.fieldsDetail1: * targets all keys from the specific depth level.
params.fieldsDetail2: In addition, the following field modifiers are also supported:
params.fieldsExcerpt: Returns a short plain text version of the field string value.
params.skipTotalDesc1: If it is set the total counts query will be skipped and the response fields totalItems and totalPages will have -1 value.
params.skipTotalDesc2: This could drastically speed up the search queries when the total counters are not needed or cursor based pagination is used.
params.skipTotalDesc3: For optimization purposes, it is set by default for the getFirstListItem() and getFullList() SDKs methods.
params.idDesc: ID of the record to view/update/delete.

apiDocs.bodyParamsNote: Body parameters could be sent as application/json or multipart/form-data.
apiDocs.fileUploadNote: File upload is supported only via multipart/form-data.
apiDocs.forMoreInfo: For more info and examples you could check the detailed
apiDocs.passwordChangeNote: Note that in case of a password change all previously issued tokens for the current record will be automatically invalidated and if you want your user to remain signed in you need to reauthenticate manually after the update call.

auth.authMethodsDesc: Returns a public list with all allowed {{name}} authentication methods.
auth.authRefreshDesc: Returns a new auth response (token and record data) for an already authenticated record.
auth.authRefreshDesc2: This method is usually called by users on page/screen reload to ensure that the previously stored data in pb.authStore is still valid and up-to-date.
auth.passwordResetNote: On successful password reset all previously issued auth tokens for the specific record will be automatically invalidated.
auth.emailChangeNote: On successful email change all previously issued auth tokens for the specific record will be automatically invalidated.
auth.emailForRequest: The auth record email address to send the request (if exists).
auth.tokenFromEmail: The token from the request email.
auth.newEmailAddress: The new email address to send the change request.
auth.accountPassword: The account password to confirm the email change.
auth.verificationToken: The token from the verification request email.

createAuthFields.emailVisibility: Whether to show/hide the auth record email when fetching the record data.
createAuthFields.verified: Indicates whether the auth record is verified or not.
createAuthFields.verifiedNote: This field can be set only by superusers or auth records with "Manage" access.

createQueryParams.expandDesc: Auto expand relations when returning the created record. Ex.:
createQueryParams.fieldsDesc: Comma separated string of the fields to return in the JSON response (by default returns all fields). Ex.:
createQueryParams.fieldsModifiersIntro: In addition, the following field modifiers are also supported:

updateAuthFields.emailUpdateNote: This field can be updated only by superusers or auth records with "Manage" access.
updateAuthFields.emailUpdateRegular: Regular accounts can update their email by calling "Request email change".
updateAuthFields.emailVisibility: Whether to show/hide the auth record email when fetching the record data.
updateAuthFields.oldPasswordNote: This field is required only when changing the record password. Superusers and auth records with "Manage" access can skip this field.
updateAuthFields.verified: Indicates whether the auth record is verified or not.
updateAuthFields.verifiedNote: This field can be set only by superusers or auth records with "Manage" access.
updateAuthFields.autoGenerated: It is autogenerated if not set.
updateAuthFields.deleteFileNote: Set to empty value (null, "", or []) to delete already uploaded file(s).

updateQueryParams.expandDesc: Auto expand relations when returning the updated record. Ex.:
updateQueryParams.fieldsDesc: Comma separated string of the fields to return in the JSON response (by default returns all fields). Ex.:
updateQueryParams.fieldsModifiersIntro: In addition, the following field modifiers are also supported:

fieldsQueryParam.description: Comma separated string of the fields to return in the JSON response
fieldsQueryParam.asteriskNote: targets all keys from the specific depth level.
fieldsQueryParam.modifiersIntro: In addition, the following field modifiers are also supported:
fieldsQueryParam.excerptDesc: Returns a short plain text version of the field string value.

fieldDescriptions.idDesc: Plain text value. It is autogenerated if not set.

realtime.title: Realtime ({{name}})
realtime.desc: Subscribe to realtime changes via Server-Sent Events (SSE).
realtime.eventsDesc: Events are sent for create, update and delete record operations (see "Event data format" section below).
realtime.subscribeNote: You could subscribe to a single record or to an entire collection.
realtime.singleRecordRule: When you subscribe to a single record, the collection's ViewRule will be used to determine whether the subscriber has access to receive the event message.
realtime.collectionRule: When you subscribe to an entire collection, the collection's ListRule will be used to determine whether the subscriber has access to receive the event message.

batch.title: Batch create/update/upsert/delete ({{name}})
batch.desc: Batch and transactional create/update/upsert/delete of multiple records in a single request.
batch.warningNote: The batch Web API need to be explicitly enabled and configured from the Dashboard settings.
batch.performanceNote: Because this endpoint process the requests in a single DB transaction it could degrade the performance of your application if not used with proper care and configuration
batch.performanceTip: (prefer smaller max processing and body size limits, avoid large file uploads over slow S3 networks and custom hooks that communicate with slow external APIs)
batch.requestsDesc: List of the requests to process.
batch.supportedActions: The supported batch request actions are:
batch.requestProperties: Each batch Request element have the following properties:
batch.customAuthNote: custom per-request Authorization header is not supported at the moment, aka. all batch requests have the same auth state
batch.multipartNote: When the batch request is send as multipart/form-data, the regular batch action fields are expected to be submitted as serialized json under the @jsonPayload field and file keys need to follow the pattern requests.N.fileField or requests[N].fileField
batch.sdkNote: (this is usually handled transparently by the SDKs when their specific object notation is used)
batch.manualFormDataNote: If you don't use the SDKs or prefer manually to construct the FormData body, then it could look something like:

backups.description: Backup and restore your PocketBase data
backups.everyFirstDay: Every first day of the month at 00:00h
backups.cronHint: Supports numeric list, steps, ranges or
backups.forcePathStyleHint: Forces the request to use path-style addressing, eg. "https://s3.amazonaws.com/BUCKET/KEY" instead of the default "https://BUCKET.s3.amazonaws.com/KEY".
backups.createDialogNote: Please note that during the backup other concurrent write requests may fail since the database will be temporary "locked" (this usually happens only during the ZIP generation).
backups.createDialogS3Note: If you are using S3 storage for the collections file upload, you'll have to backup them separately since they are not locally stored and will not be included in the final backup!
backups.restoreWarning: Please proceed with caution and use it only with trusted backups!
backups.restoreExperimental: Backup restore is experimental and works only on UNIX based systems.
backups.restoreReplaceNote: The restore operation will attempt to replace your existing
backups.restoreReplaceNote2: with the one from the backup and will restart the application process.
backups.restoreDataNote: This means that on success all of your data (including app settings, users, superusers, etc.) will be replaced with the ones from the backup.
backups.restoreInvalidNote: Nothing will happen if the backup is invalid (ex. missing
backups.restoreFlowTitle: Below is an oversimplified version of the restore flow:
backups.restoreStep1b: with the content from the backup
backups.restoreStep3: Applies all migrations that are missing in the restored
backups.restoreStep4: Initializes the app server as usual
backups.uploadDialogDesc: Note that we don't perform validations for the uploaded backup files. Proceed with caution and only if you trust the source.
backups.uploadConfirm: Do you really want to upload "{{name}}"?
backups.deleteConfirm: Do you really want to delete {{name}}?

analyticsSettings.s3BucketTooltip: Optional S3 bucket for storing raw analytics logs. Required for PostgreSQL mode if you want to preserve raw event data.

backupRestore.title: Restore Backup
backupRestore.warning: Please proceed with caution and use only trusted backups!
backupRestore.experimental: Backup restore is experimental and works only on UNIX based systems.
backupRestore.replaceNote: The restore operation will attempt to replace your existing pb_data directory with the content from the backup and restart the application process.
backupRestore.allDataReplace: This means that after success, all data (including application settings, users, superusers, etc.) will be replaced with the backup data.
backupRestore.invalidNote: Nothing will happen if the backup is invalid (e.g. missing data.db file).
backupRestore.flowTitle: Below is a simplified version of the restore flow:
backupRestore.step3: Apply missing migrations in the restored pb_data
backupRestore.restoreError: Backup restore operation failed, please retry

apiRules.availableFieldsTitle: The following record fields are available:
apiRules.requestFieldsTitle: The request fields could be accessed with the special
apiRules.collectionFilterTitle: You could also add constraints and query other collections using the
apiRules.authRuleHelp1: This rule is executed every time before authentication allowing you to restrict who can authenticate.
apiRules.authRuleHelp2: For example, to allow only verified users you can set it to
apiRules.authRuleHelp3: Leave it empty to allow anyone with an account to authenticate.
apiRules.authRuleHelp4: To disable authentication entirely you can change it to "Set superusers only".
apiRules.manageRuleHelp1: This rule is executed in addition to the
apiRules.manageRuleHelp2: It enables superuser-like permissions to allow fully managing the auth record(s), eg. changing the password without requiring to enter the old one, directly updating the verified state or email, etc.

trustedProxy.title: Trusted Proxy
trustedProxy.recommendTrust: It is recommend to list it as trusted.
trustedProxy.desc1: When PocketBase is deployed on platforms like Fly or it is accessible through proxies such as NGINX, requests from different users will originate from the same IP address (the IP of the proxy connecting to your PocketBase app).
trustedProxy.desc2: In this case to retrieve the actual user IP (used for rate limiting, logging, etc.) you need to properly configure your proxy and list below the trusted headers that PocketBase could use to extract the user IP.
trustedProxy.desc3: When using such proxy, to avoid spoofing it is recommended to:
trustedProxy.tip1: use headers that are controlled only by the proxy and cannot be manually set by the users
trustedProxy.tip2: make sure that the PocketBase server can be accessed only through the proxy
trustedProxy.clearHint: You can clear the headers field if PocketBase is not deployed behind a proxy.
trustedProxy.ipPriorityTooltip: This is in case the proxy returns more than 1 IP as header value. The rightmost IP is usually considered to be the more trustworthy but this could vary depending on the proxy.

rateLimits.title: Rate Limits
rateLimits.learnMore: Learn more about the rate limit rules
rateLimits.infoP1: The rate limit rules are resolved in the following order (stops on the first match):
rateLimits.infoP2: In case of multiple rules with the same label but different target user audience (e.g. "guest" vs "auth"), only the matching audience rule is taken in consideration.
rateLimits.infoP3: The rate limit label could be in one of the following formats:

batchRequests.title: Batch Requests
batchRequests.maxRequestsTooltip: Rate limiting (if enabled) also applies for the batch create/update/upsert/delete requests.
batchRequests.hideControls: Hide collection create and edit controls
batchRequests.hideControlsTooltip: This could prevent making accidental schema changes when in production environment.
